Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search for contact details for Grace Yamamoto, Denise Holloway, and Raymond Chen using contacts_search_contacts (or similar) to retrieve their email addresses (grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com, denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com, raymond.chen@keystonemortgage.com) needed for the compliance summary email and for scoping email searches.
OE 2: Search the mortgage LOS for loans in clear-to-close or active processing status using mortgage_los_get_pipeline or mortgage_los_search_loans with a status filter for "clear_to_close" (or similar) to identify the set of loans approaching closing that need compliance verification. Expect approximately 17 loans in clear_to_close status.
OE 3: For each loan identified in OE 2, retrieve conditions using mortgage_los_get_conditions with the loan_id parameter to check whether conditions marked as cleared actually have supporting documentation the same pattern Raymond flagged in the QC review.
OE 4: For each loan approaching closing, retrieve the loan record using mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id (or mortgage_los_get_loan) to check TRID compliance by comparing the cd_sent_date field against the closing_date field. The Closing Disclosure must be sent at least 3 business days before closing. Flag any loans where cd_sent_date is null, missing, or too close to closing_date.
OE 5: Search emails for TRID or Closing Disclosure timing issues using search_emails with queries like "Thompson CD timing" or "TRID" or "closing disclosure" (or similar) to find the known CD timing discrepancy on the Thompson file - specifically Denise Holloway's audit notes flagging that the LOS CD date does not match the email trail or document metadata, Denise's escalation to Robert Calloway about the unresolved discrepancy, Elena Marchetti's heads-up email to Grace warning that the Thompson CD did not go out in the full 3-day window, and Grace's reply to Denise explaining she could not reconcile the timeline.
OE 6: Search Slack messages for TRID and CD timing discussions using conversations_search_messages with queries like "Thompson" or "CD" or "TRID" (or similar) to find Elena's DM to Grace flagging that the Thompson CD did not go out in the full 3-day window, Denise's audit note in #compliance-alerts, and Grace's comment in #loan-processing about not being able to reconcile the LOS timestamps.
OE 7: Search emails for Raymond's QC findings using search_emails with queries like "QC exceptions" or "Raymond" or "conditions cleared" (or similar) to find Raymond's email to Grace identifying three loans (LN-2026-00603 for Heather Sullivan closed, LN-2026-00211, and LN-2026-00224) where Tyler Washington cleared conditions without supporting documents in the file.
OE 8: Search emails for the system outage and ransomware incident using search_emails with queries like "system outage" or "ransomware" or "LOS down" (or similar) to find Grace's company-wide "Temporary systems outage" email, Raj's escalation confirming ransomware on the LOS environment with a 2 BTC ransom demand, Raj's preliminary finding tying the entry point to a phishing email, and Denise's engagement of cyber counsel Megan Sloane.
OE 9: Search Slack messages for the system outage and incident response using conversations_search_messages or conversations_history on channels like #it-support (C008) and #loan-processing (C002) with queries like "ransomware" or "outage" or "LOS down" (or similar) to find Raj's initial alert, Grace's request for a processing read on Monday closings at risk (LN-2026-00601 plus Reyes and Dalton files), and the incident command thread timeline.
OE 10: Search CRM engagements for notes related to the QC findings and the system incident using crm_list_engagements (or similar) to find engagement notes documenting the QC spot check findings (crm_engagement_221f612776cd, crm_engagement_b10a0d08f620), Tyler's post-meeting admission that conditions were cleared before docs were uploaded (crm_engagement_09f265e4e5f0), the pending decision on conduct vs. training gap vs. staffing/process failure, and the system outage notes (crm_engagement_ca7760e8f9d5, crm_engagement_2b9c91c10337) documenting the LOS encryption event and incident escalation.
OE 11: For each loan approaching closing, verify borrower fee collection in Stripe. First use stripe_search_customers with borrower name or email (or similar) to find the Stripe customer_id for each borrower, then use stripe_list_charges filtered by that customer_id (or stripe_search_charges with customer_id query) to check that appraisal fees, credit report fees, and application deposits have been collected. Flag any loans with missing or failed charges.
OE 12: For the three QC-flagged loans (LN-2026-00603, LN-2026-00211, LN-2026-00224), retrieve each loan record using mortgage_los_get_loan with the loan_number (or mortgage_los_search_loans) to check current status and whether any remediation has occurred since the QC findings were reported. Note: Raymond's email describes LN-2026-00603 (Heather Sullivan) as closed, LN-2026-00211 as open, and LN-2026-00224 as open. The LOS records show LN-2026-00603 status as "processing", LN-2026-00211 as "withdrawn", and LN-2026-00224 as "closed" - the agent should report what the LOS shows as the source of truth.
OE 13: Send an email (via send_email) from tyler.washington@keystonemortgage.com to grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com and denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com with a consolidated compliance summary covering: TRID timing findings (including the Thompson CD discrepancy), documentation gaps from the QC review (the three loans with conditions cleared without supporting docs), borrower fee collection status from Stripe, and any data integrity concerns related to the system outage. Flag items needing immediate attention before the next closing.
OE 14: Add a compliance review note to each loan file where an issue was found using mortgage_los_add_activity with the loan_id and a description noting the specific issue (e.g., "TRID CD timing discrepancy flagged during compliance review" for the Thompson file, "Conditions cleared without supporting documentation flagged in QC review" for LN-2026-00603, LN-2026-00211, and LN-2026-00224, and similar notes for any loans with missing Stripe fees or outage-related data concerns).
OE 15: Save a full written compliance review report to the file system using filesystem write tools (or similar) with a path such as "compliance_review_report.txt" (or similar) containing the complete findings across all areas - TRID results, QC condition documentation audit, Stripe fee verification, and system outage impact assessment - so the report is documented for the next QC audit.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

✗Run #6Although the agent found the ransomware reference in a Slack message ('This is ransomware, not just LOS down'), it never sent an email or saved a written report, so this finding was not surfaced in either required output.

query construction

✗Run #2The mortgage_los_get_pipeline calls were made with only a limit parameter (limit: 50) or no parameters at all. No status filter (e.g., 'clear_to_close', 'CTC', 'closing') was applied to narrow the pipeline to closing-ready loans.
✗Run #3The mortgage_los_get_pipeline call used only {"limit": 50} with no status filter for 'clear_to_close,' 'CTC,' 'closing,' or any closing-related status. The agent did not filter the pipeline by closing status.
✗Run #4The mortgage_los_get_pipeline call used only {"limit": 50} with no explicit status filter for 'clear_to_close', 'CTC', or similar. The pipeline returned happened to include CTC loans but no status filter was applied in the query parameters.
✗Run #5The mortgage_los_get_pipeline call used only a limit:50 parameter with no status filter for clear_to_close, CTC, closing, or similar closing-related status values.
✓Run #6The agent explicitly called mortgage_los_get_pipeline with the parameter status='clear_to_close', satisfying the requirement to filter by a closing-readiness status.
